note: counts are 'at least' thresholds, not exact — new edges landing on Tywin (a hub) between sessions shouldn't break this case. Live snapshot 2026-07-04: outgoingCount=80, incomingCount=46.
asserts the exact ordered target-slug set for one specific, stable, low-volatility edge type/direction (Tywin's three children) rather than the whole 80-link outgoing set.

expect.assertion: outgoingCount == sum(len(v) for v in outgoing.values()); incomingCount == sum(len(v) for v in incoming.values())

note: structural invariant: the reported counts must equal what the grouped/deduped output actually shows (a duped neighbour is not a second connection). Restates graph_test.ts's dedup-consistency check as a shared case.
trust-boundary check (validate.ts isValidSlug) rejects the malformed slug before any lookup; must not throw.
